channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: Relatives say an autistic and nonverbal teenage boy who was shot by Idaho police from the other side of a chain link fence while holding a knife has died.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle And Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump undergoes his annual physical after years of reluctance to share medical information
item.description: Donald Trump had his annual physical on Friday, a check-up that may give the public its first details in years about the health of a man who, in January, became the oldest in U.S. history to be sworn in as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Regulators order corrective action as Keystone Pipeline operators aim to restore service
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — Federal regulators have ordered the operator of the Keystone Pipeline to take several corrective actions after a rupture caused 147,000 gallons of oil to spill onto farmland in North Dakota, and the company said its goal is to resume deliveries to refineries on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Judge relaxes ban on DOGE access to sensitive US Treasury information
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge has relaxed a ban she’d put on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency preventing it from accessing sensitive Treasury Department information related to millions of Americans. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kansas man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration ordered to unfreeze funding in dispute with Maine over transgender students
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to unfreeze funds intended for a Maine child nutrition program that were suspended amid a disagreement between the state and the president over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawyers for Sean 'Diddy' Combs want jurors at trial questioned about sex, drugs and violence
item.description: Lawyers for Sean “Diddy” Combs want a judge to let prospective jurors at his upcoming sex trafficking trial be questioned about sex, drugs and violence.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about activist Mahmoud Khalil and his attorneys' plan to appeal his deportation ruling
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge has ruled that a Palestinian Columbia University graduate student who participated in protests against Israel can be deported. Mahmoud Khalil's attorneys said they will appeal Friday's ruling.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to target chronic disease in US tribes. A key program to do that was gutted
item.description: CHANDLER, Ariz. (AP) — Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. spent time in tribal communities in Arizona and New Mexico this week highlighting ways they are trying to prevent chronic disease among Native Americans and Alaska Natives, something he has said is one of his top priorities.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE, GRAHAM LEE BREWER and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina executes second man by firing squad in 5 weeks
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A firing squad on Friday executed a South Carolina man who killed an off-duty police officer, the second time the rare execution method has been used by the state in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rachel Morin murder: Illegal immigrant suspect denied ever being in Maryland at time of crime
item.description: The murder trial of Victor Antonio Martinez-Hernandez, the Salvadoran illegal immigrant accused of murdering Bel-Air woman Rachel Morin in August 2023, will continue Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Kohberger's evidence fight, Vallow's latest trial, Read's jury selection
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Florida universities to deputize campus police for immigration enforcement
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The police departments of at least three public universities in Florida are seeking agreements with the federal government to carry out immigration enforcement on campus.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon’s network includes millions of third-party sellers who are either in China or whose products originate from there.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Plane crash near Florida highway kills 3 on board, injures 1 on the ground
item.description: A spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said the plane, a Cessna 310, crashed at around 10:20 a.m. after departing from the nearby airport.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Family from Spain, pilot killed in NYC sightseeing helicopter crash
item.description: Photos posted on the helicopter company’s website showed the couple and their children smiling as they boarded just before the flight took off.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Utah labor leaders in final push to repeal controversial collective bargaining ban
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Labor leaders are racing against the clock as they now have less than a week to get enough signatures to place a referendum on the ballot to overturn one of this year’s most controversial bills – H.B. 267.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, ABC 4

item.title: USPS warns about common scams
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service and Postal Inspection Service want to warn consumers to be aware of fraud and spam regarding their mail, and what to do to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Vietnam veteran finds PTSD relief with RTM therapy
item.description: RTM therapy is gaining popularity as a PTSD treatment, with a reportedly high success rate and little risk of relapse.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump nominee for public lands post withdraws after her criticism of Jan. 6 attack surfaces
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to oversee an agency that manages a quarter-billion acres of public land has withdrawn her nomination following revelations that she criticized the Republican president in 2021 for inciting the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mourners pay tribute to merengue icon Rubby Pérez, who died in the Dominican roof collapse
item.description: Fans and countrymen on Thursday shared their remembrances of Dominican Republic music icon Rubby Pérez, who was among the scores of people killed this week after the roof at the Jet Set club in Santo Domingo collapsed. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, KRON4

item.title: Students react to nearly 50 international students' visas being revoked, records being removed across Utah
item.description: International students at institutions across Utah and around the country are having their records terminated or their visas revoked. Here's what some students said about it.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
